source_jobs.title: Director of Content Strategy
source_jobs.description:
Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup that has raised over $30M from Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures. Based in San Francisco, the company was founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by automating disputes and fighting friendly fraud. People https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241030764010/en/Money2020-USA-Unveils-Seven-Incredible-Fintech-Startups-And-Industry-Disruptors love https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition what we’ve built, from everyday users to the biggest names in finance.

Casap is looking for a Director of Content Strategy excited to drive compelling narratives that educate financial institution leaders about a rapidly scaling product at the forefront of automation and artificial intelligence.

Responsibilities

 - Develop and execute content strategy across all channels, translating complex fraud and banking concepts into clear narratives that resonate with credit union and bank leaders at different buying stages.

 - Create high-impact content including decks, one-sheeters, thought leadership, video content, and customer testimonials that drive awareness and conversion.

 - Own content planning and campaign development in collaboration with sales, product, and executive teams to align messaging with business objectives.

 - Collaborate with CEO and subject matter experts to craft thought leadership positioning Casap as an authority in the space.

 - Manage external creative partners (agencies, freelancers) while maintaining strategic oversight and quality standards.

 - Use data and performance insights to optimize content effectiveness and inform future initiatives.

Qualifications

 - 4-8 years of experience in content strategy, brand storytelling, or marketing communications, preferably in fintech, financial services, or B2B technology.

 - Exceptional writing and content creation skills with a portfolio demonstrating ability to simplify complex technical concepts for executive audiences.

 - Experience creating executive-facing materials including presentation decks, one-sheeters, and thought leadership content.

 - Strong strategic thinking with experience translating business concepts into multi-format content campaigns that drive measurable results.

 - Comfort working in fast-paced startup environments with high autonomy and cross-functional collaboration.

 - Experience managing external creative resources and using data to optimize content performance.

Compensation: $125,000-$175,000 base salary + equity, based on experience

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASAP

Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup that has raised over $30M from Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures. Based in San Francisco, the company was founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by automating disputes and fighting friendly fraud. People https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241030764010/en/Money2020-USA-Unveils-Seven-Incredible-Fintech-Startups-And-Industry-Disruptors love https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition what we’ve built, from everyday users to the biggest names in finance.

Casap is looking for a talented Machine Learning Engineer in San Francisco excited to supercharge how banks operate with a rapidly scaling product at the forefront of automation and Artificial Intelligence.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and implement machine learning models to evaluate disputes and chargebacks and likelihood of fraud

 - Create and manage an orchestration layer for multiple models, enabling automated and supervised decision-making processes

 - Collaborate with stakeholders to leverage valuable data from partners and customers, ensuring a continuously learning experience and first-class user experience
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of experience designing and deploying ML models in a high-scale production environment.

 - Strong knowledge of machine learning algorithms and data analysis techniques.

 - Enthusiastic about building ML infrastructure from scratch in a nascent environment.

 - Ability to think like a product engineer, balancing technical innovation with practical application and user needs.

 - Strong proficiency in programming languages like Python, R, or similar, and experience with ML frameworks like TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit-learn.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently and in a collaborative team environment.

source_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASAP

Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup that has raised over $30M from Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures. Based in San Francisco, the company was founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by automating disputes and fighting friendly fraud. People https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241030764010/en/Money2020-USA-Unveils-Seven-Incredible-Fintech-Startups-And-Industry-Disruptors love https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition what we’ve built, from everyday users to the biggest names in finance.

Casap is seeking talented Technical Program Managers to join our teams in San Francisco and New York City, excited to supercharge how banks operate with a rapidly scaling product at the forefront of automation and Artificial Intelligence.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for new customers from post-sale through go-live, acting as the “face of Casap” during onboarding.

 - Lead technical kickoff calls, walking clients through the implementation plan and aligning on scope, timeline, and integration needs.

 - Translate client requirements (e.g. stack, fraud workflows, capabilities) into clear technical specs for the engineering team.

 - Coordinate with client-side IT, InfoSec, and fraud teams to drive integrations forward and address security or infrastructure needs.

 - Partner closely with internal engineering and product teams to scope work, remove blockers, and keep implementations on track.

 - Manage all onboarding meetings and communications with a white-glove, enterprise-ready approach.

 - Represent Casap with professionalism and executive presence when working with financial institutions, credit unions, and fintechs.

 - Ensure a smooth handoff to the platform support team once clients are fully live and integrated.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of experience in technical project management, solutions engineering, or customer implementation roles, ideally in SaaS or fintech.

 - Strong technical fluency — able to understand APIs, data workflows, and customer environments, and translate requirements for engineering teams.

 - Direct experience working with enterprise clients, especially in financial services, fintech, or other regulated industries.

 - Proven track record managing complex, cross-functional projects involving product, engineering, and external stakeholders.

 - Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage both technical and executive-level stakeholders.

 - Highly organized and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple projects in fast-paced environments.

 - Bonus: Familiarity with fraud systems, payment infrastructure, or chargeback processes!

source_jobs.title: Director of Growth (Senior Strategic BDR)
source_jobs.description:
Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup based in San Francisco founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by building an autonomous banking platform from the ground up. We’re starting with automating dispute and chargeback resolution and already, customers and the industry https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition love our product. Casap is backed by Lightspeed Ventures, Primary Ventures, and a variety of strategic investors and partners.

Casap is looking for a Director of Growth to help build relationships and source sales opportunities with Bank, Credit Union, and Fintech leaders.

This isn’t your traditional high volume BDR role. You will partner directly with a Senior Account Executive on our team to craft strategic outreach to a focused list of large strategic accounts. Yes, you’ll make calls and send emails, but never generic bulk messages. All thoughtful. You’ll travel to events and even create and host your own to break into the right accounts.

There’s tremendous demand for Casap’s product. We just need to educate the market so we’re top of mind when the timing’s right for them to evaluate.

Responsibilities

 - Partner directly with a Senior Account Executive to develop and execute strategic outreach campaigns for a focused list of large strategic accounts in your territory

 - Conduct thoughtful, low-volume cold calling and email outreach to bank, credit union, and fintech executives - never generic bulk messages

 - Travel 1-2 times per month to attend industry conferences, trade shows, and networking events to build relationships and generate pipeline

 - Create and host your own events such as dinner series, customer roundtables, and executive briefings to break into target accounts

 - Work closely with your paired AE on account strategy, messaging, and relationship building for complex sales cycles

 - Build and maintain relationships with prospects over 6-12 month timeframes, staying top-of-mind for when timing is right

 - Collaborate on account planning and territory strategy to maximize coverage of high-value prospects

 - Track and report on relationship-building activities, pipeline generation, and territory development metrics

Qualifications

 - You could really be anyone, BUT most likely you have same sales experience (even if selling something totally unrelated to what Casap does)

 - Being the kind of person who can build relationships with the proverbial “janitor and CEO”

 - Self-starter mentality with ability to operate with high autonomy in sometimes-ambiguous startup environment

 - "Pirate ship" mentality - hungry, resourceful, and able to succeed without big company brand recognition

 - Comfortable on the phones and at in-person events with executives

 - Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills with natural charisma and executive presence

 - Experience selling large deals to bigger companies with long sales cycles is a plus, but not required

 - Organized with systems-oriented approach to relationship management and follow-up

 - Comfort with travel (1-2 times per month)

source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff - CEO
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASAP

Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup that has raised over $30M from Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures. Based in San Francisco, the company was founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by automating disputes and fighting friendly fraud. People https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241030764010/en/Money2020-USA-Unveils-Seven-Incredible-Fintech-Startups-And-Industry-Disruptors love https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition what we’ve built, from everyday users to the biggest names in finance.

Reporting directly to the CEO, the Chief of Staff (CoS) will be the CEO’s right hand, owning strategic planning, operating rhythm, and special projects that keep Casap running smoothly during hyper-growth. You’ll turn big ideas into structured workstreams, remove blockers for the leadership team, and ensure we hit our ambitious goals.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as a thought partner to the CEO, and serve as CEO-extension in San Francisco (CEO will be based in NY)

 - Support go-to-market initiatives including managing conferences and speaking engagements, and help support GTM motion

 - Own internal comms: run exec staff meetings, publish company-wide updates, and champion transparency.

 - Drive quarterly and annual planning (OKRs, budgets) and track execution across Product, GTM, and Operations.

 - Prepare board meetings, investor updates, and key metrics dashboards.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–5 years in management consulting, investment banking, strategy/ops at a high-growth tech company, or similar; MBA preferred.

 - Proven ability to move from 30,000-ft strategy to 3-ft execution.

 - Very strong communication skills (both written and verbal), and executive presence (very comfortable speaking in public)

 - Super attention to details and crisp business writing.

 - Low-ego collaborator comfortable influencing senior leaders.

 - Passion for fintech, fraud prevention, or compliance software is a bonus.

source_jobs.title: Director of Strategic Relationships (Senior Account Executive)
source_jobs.description:
Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup based in San Francisco founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by building an autonomous banking platform from the ground up. We’re starting with automating dispute and chargeback resolution and already, customers and the industry https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition love our product. Casap is backed by Lightspeed Ventures, Primary Ventures, and a variety of strategic investors and partners.

Casap is looking for a Senior AE (official job title: Director of Strategic Relationships) to own and close deals with our most strategic bank, credit union, and fintech prospects.

This is a rare opportunity to seize a market at exactly the right moment. Customers genuinely love our product, some even use “Casap” as a verb. With strong product-market fit, and a powerful pipeline generation engine in place, you’ll focus on working qualified, high-value opportunities through the sales process. You'll work hand-in-hand with a dedicated Senior Strategic BDR (our Directors of Growth) on a focused territory of strategic accounts and they’ll lead pipeline generation.

We're not looking for someone who needs hand-holding - we want someone with a pirate ship mentality who can operate without big company brand recognition and build something meaningful in financial services.



Responsibilities

 - Own and manage a strategic territory (East or Central) of high-value bank, credit union, and fintech prospects

 - Close high ACV deals with longer sales cycles at top-tier financial institutions

 - Partner closely with a Director of Growth to develop account strategy and work qualified opportunities through the sales process

 - Conduct strategic discovery calls, product demonstrations, and executive-level presentations

 - Travel 1-2 times per month for in-person customer meetings, events, and relationship building

 - Build and maintain relationships with C-level and VP-level executives at large financial institutions

 - Navigate complex sales processes, multi-stakeholder decision-making, and robust approval processes, at enterprise accounts

 - Collaborate with internal teams to ensure successful customer onboarding and expansion opportunities

 - Contribute to sales process refinement as the company scales

 - Participate in industry conferences and customer events to build market presence
   
   

Qualifications

 - Multiple years of B2B sales experience with a track record of closing mid-six-figure deals

 - Proven experience selling into large financial services accounts (banks, credit unions, fintechs)

 - Strong discovery and consultative selling skills with ability to build business cases for complex solutions

 - Experience navigating longer sales sales cycles with multiple stakeholders and decision makers is a plus

 - Exceptional presentation and communication skills with C-level executives

 - Self-starter mentality with ability to operate with high autonomy in startup environment

 - "Pirate ship" mentality - hungry, resourceful, and able to succeed without big company brand recognition

 - Comfortable with travel (1-2 times per month) for customer meetings

 - Financial services industry knowledge preferred but not required
   
   

Compensation: OTE $270k + equity (we expect strong performers to meaningfully exceed OTE)

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASAP

Casap https://www.casaphq.com is building the AI agents that will run the modern bank.

We are a Series A company backed by Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures, founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. Our mission is to replace manual, rules-based back office workflows with intelligent agents that detect fraud, assess risk, and resolve disputes autonomously. To ultimately create a future where trust is a given and fraud is rare.

Today, banks still rely on fragmented tools, static rules, and large operations teams to manage fraud and disputes. Casap is changing that. Our platform uses agentic AI to reason over transactions, customer data, and network signals to make real-time decisions—reducing losses, protecting honest customers, and giving financial institutions a smarter, faster way to operate.

We've seen incredible market traction and our customers love us https://www.linkedin.com/posts/shanthi-shanmugam-4057284a_last-week-at-filene-research-institutes-activity-7404160615948132352-jBup?rcm=ACoAAApuDkUBC-QU04abePh55zc_0i-kRnbmke4&utm_medium=member_desktop&utm_source=share.

We’re now looking for a Senior Product Manager to help define and scale the next generation of AI-powered risk and fraud agents. You’ll own complex, high-impact product areas, work directly with engineering, data, and customers, and shape how autonomous systems make decisions in regulated financial environments.

If you’re excited by:

 - Building AI products that operate at massive scale

 - Turning messy, high-stakes workflows into autonomous systems

 - Designing for trust, explainability, and real business impact

…this is the kind of problem—and company—you’ll want to help build.

Casap is based in San Francisco and New York City, and we’re growing fast.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create and maintain a clear product roadmap - with well-defined, measurable objectives that align with business goals and customer needs.

 - Work cross-functionally with engineering, marketing, revenue, design, and other stakeholders to develop, validate, and prioritize new features and experiences.

 - Own execution of the roadmap - driving results while obsessing over details that delight users without letting “perfect” get in the way of progress.

 - Develop a deep understanding of our users through a combination of qualitative research (e.g. user interviews, usability studies) and quantitative analysis (e.g. usage metrics, analytics).

 - Rapidly experiment - design and run A/B tests and other experiments to validate hypotheses and iterate toward the right solutions.

 - Act as a product advocate across the organization, aligning teams around product vision, tradeoffs, and priorities.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of experience in product management (ideally SaaS, marketplace, or consumer/enterprise-facing products).

 - Results-driven: you set clear criteria for success, track outcomes, and iterate based on what you learn.

 - Excellent communicator - able to bridge technical and non-technical stakeholders and clearly articulate product vision, requirements, tradeoffs, and strategy.

 - Strong organizational and time-management skills; capable of juggling multiple initiatives, aligning cross-functional partners, and delivering on deadlines.

 - Passionate about building great products - empathetic toward users, curious about their needs, and driven to solve real problems.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, dynamic environment; you can move quickly without sacrificing quality.

 - Bonus: Familiarity with fraud systems, payment infrastructure, or chargeback processes!

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASAP

Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup that has raised over $30M from Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures. Based in San Francisco, the company was founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by automating disputes and fighting friendly fraud. People https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241030764010/en/Money2020-USA-Unveils-Seven-Incredible-Fintech-Startups-And-Industry-Disruptors love https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition what we’ve built, from everyday users to the biggest names in finance.

Casap is looking for talented Backend Engineers (~10) in San Francisco excited to supercharge how banks operate with a rapidly scaling product at the forefront of automation and Artificial Intelligence.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and maintain reliable backend systems, integrating them with credit card, banking, and merchant platforms for comprehensive workflow chargeback management.

 - Scale and generalize the system for growth, ensuring it is highly configurable and flexible to reduce the time to go live and support a broad range of use cases.

 - Architect and develop robust architectural patterns for microservices.

 - Design and implement core frameworks and libraries for various backend services, promoting code reuse and maintainability.

 - Take ownership of upgrading coding practices, CI/CD pipelines, deployed systems, backend programming languages, and third-party dependencies to enhance system performance and security.

 - Proactively identify areas for improvement in our platform's infrastructure and systems, implementing optimizations that enhance operational efficiency and product quality.

 - Own the end-to-end development and deployment of key features, including decision-making around technology stack and system design to ensure optimal implementation.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Minimum of 6+ years of experience in software development, with at least 2 years focused on using Go or Java in production environments.

 - Demonstrated experience working on platform teams and contributing significantly to system architecture.

 - Led an end-to-end implementation of a key product feature or re-architecture/upgrading of a key platform service.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills with a strong understanding of fundamentals, algorithms, data structures, and industry design patterns.

 - Experience mentoring junior team members and leading project teams as the sole tech lead.

 - Strong communication skills and ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment.

source_jobs.title: Head of Implementations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASAP

Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup that has raised over $30M from Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures. Based in San Francisco, the company was founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by automating disputes and fighting friendly fraud. People https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241030764010/en/Money2020-USA-Unveils-Seven-Incredible-Fintech-Startups-And-Industry-Disruptors love https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition what we’ve built, from everyday users to the biggest names in finance.

Casap is looking for a Head of Implementations excited to drive seamless onboarding and integration experiences for financial institutions as we scale our platform at the forefront of automation and Artificial Intelligence.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and lead the team: Scale the implementations organization (5-10+ Implementation Managers) by hiring exceptional technical project managers and future implementation leaders

 - Own implementation strategy: Design end-to-end implementation methodologies, establish scalable processes, and ensure customers go live quickly without compromising quality or security

 - Create customer-centric culture: Build a culture of ownership, accountability, technical excellence, and deep customer empathy within the implementations team

 - Drive consistent delivery: Establish implementation frameworks, success metrics, and playbooks that enable predictable time-to-value across all customer segments

 - Mentor and coach: Support Implementation Managers through coaching, feedback, and professional development to elevate technical and project management capabilities

 - Partner cross-functionally: Work closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to deliver comprehensive solutions and drive operational excellence across the customer lifecycle


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Proven track record of building and scaling technical implementation teams at B2B SaaS companies, preferably in fintech or financial services

 - 8+ years in technical implementations, solutions engineering, or professional services, with 3+ years in a leadership role

 - 5+ years managing complex enterprise implementations involving APIs, data integrations, and financial systems

 - Led multiple end-to-end implementations of banking software or fintech platforms for Tier 1 financial institutions

 - Expert understanding of financial services technology, banking operations, API integrations, and enterprise security requirements

 - Strong experience mentoring implementation leaders and building high-performing customer-facing teams

 - Excellent communication skills with ability to influence technical decisions and build executive relationships with C-level banking stakeholders


WHY THIS ROLE

 - Rare opportunity to own and scale implementations from day one as we expand from top fintech brands to traditional banks

 - Seat at the table — you'll be a true strategic partner to the CEO, founders, and go-to-market leadership

 - Clear path to grow with the company as we scale through Series B and beyond, potentially expanding into VP of Customer Operations or Chief Customer Officer

 - A high-trust, no-ego culture of doers, thinkers, and builders

 - Backed by top-tier investors

source_jobs.title: Head of Customer Success
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASAP

Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup that has raised over $30M from Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures. Based in San Francisco, the company was founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by automating disputes and fighting friendly fraud. People https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241030764010/en/Money2020-USA-Unveils-Seven-Incredible-Fintech-Startups-And-Industry-Disruptors love https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition what we’ve built, from everyday users to the biggest names in finance.

Casap is seeking a Head of Customer Success to lead the full post-onboarding customer journey and build a world-class Customer Success function from the ground up. You’ll own the strategy and execution across customer success, support, renewals, and account growth—driving outcomes that maximize customer satisfaction and retention. You’ll collaborate closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Implementation to ensure our customers extract maximum value from Casap.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the Customer Journey: Lead the entire post-implementation lifecycle for our customers—from initial handoff through renewals and upsells—ensuring a seamless and high-impact experience at every step.

 - Build & Scale the CS Org: Hire, manage, and mentor a growing team across customer success and support. Implement scalable structures, playbooks, and KPIs to support Casap’s rapid growth.

 - Drive Strategic Outcomes: Build deep relationships with customers, understand their evolving needs, and proactively drive product adoption, account expansion, and long-term loyalty.

 - Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Partner closely with Product, Engineering, and Sales to surface customer insights, influence roadmap priorities, and continuously improve the customer experience.

 - Elevate Support Operations: Develop a high-performing support function with clear SLAs, fast resolution times, and a white-glove experience for our financial institution clients.

 - Build Systems & Processes: Define success metrics, reporting cadences, and operational workflows that help scale the team while maintaining quality and responsiveness.

 - Act as a Trusted Advisor: Represent Casap with executive presence in customer conversations, earning the trust of key stakeholders across banks, credit unions, and fintechs.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of experience in B2B SaaS Customer Success, with a strong track record in high-touch, strategic accounts—ideally mid-market and enterprise customers.

 - Proven ability to build and scale post-sales functions, including hiring, org design, and process development.

 - Analytical and metrics-driven, with experience managing to net retention, CSAT, and support SLAs.

 - Deep understanding of customer success best practices and playbooks for early-stage SaaS companies.

 - Background in fintech or working with financial institutions; experience with the office of the CFO is a strong plus.

 - Familiarity with ERPs (e.g., QuickBooks, NetSuite) and/or financial systems integration workflows.

 - Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills; comfortable engaging both technical and executive-level contacts.

 - Hands-on, scrappy, and excited to build in a fast-paced startup environment!

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CASAP

Casap https://www.casaphq.com/ is a Series A startup that has raised over $30M from Emergence, Lightspeed, and Primary Ventures. Based in San Francisco, the company was founded by product leaders from Robinhood and Chime. We are on a mission to change the way banks operate by automating disputes and fighting friendly fraud. People https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20241030764010/en/Money2020-USA-Unveils-Seven-Incredible-Fintech-Startups-And-Industry-Disruptors love https://www.cubroadcast.com/episodes/nacuso24-casaps-dispute-solution-wins-2024-next-big-idea-competition what we’ve built, from everyday users to the biggest names in finance.

We’re looking for a Customer Success Manager to join our growing team and serve as a trusted advisor to Casap’s customers. In this role, you’ll manage ongoing relationships with banks, credit unions, and fintechs—ensuring a seamless post-onboarding experience, driving adoption, and helping customers realize the full value of Casap. You’ll collaborate closely with our Implementation, Product, Engineering, and Sales teams to advocate for customers and help shape the future of our platform.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage Customer Relationships: Own a portfolio of customer accounts from go-live through renewal—building strong relationships, ensuring satisfaction, and acting as their day-to-day partner.

 - Drive Product Adoption: Guide customers through onboarding milestones, usage best practices, and product updates to maximize impact and long-term success.

 - Deliver a White-Glove Experience: Serve as a proactive resource and problem solver—resolving issues quickly and professionally, with an enterprise-ready approach.

 - Support Growth & Retention: Monitor customer health, identify risks or upsell opportunities, and partner with Sales to support renewals and expansions.

 - Advocate for Customers Internally: Gather product feedback, surface customer pain points, and work cross-functionally with Product and Engineering to improve the customer experience.

 - Contribute to CS Playbooks: Help build and improve internal processes, knowledge bases, and engagement strategies to scale customer success at Casap.

 - Represent Casap with Credibility: Communicate confidently with both technical users and executive stakeholders across financial institutions.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–6 years of experience in a Customer Success, Account Management, or Customer Experience role at a B2B SaaS company.

 - Strong relationship management skills with a track record of supporting mid-market or enterprise clients in high-touch environments.

 - Experience working with technical products or integrations, ideally in fintech, regtech, or working with financial institutions.

 - Familiarity with APIs, ERPs (e.g., NetSuite, QuickBooks), or financial system workflows is a plus.

 - Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple customers and priorities in a fast-paced, early-stage environment.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills with a professional and empathetic tone.

 - Passion for great customer experiences and excitement about building a function from the ground up!

notes: Fintech startup with a strong focus on product and engineering roles.
